messages.content: I worked out that GCD(2014, 1653) = 19. Did I get it right?

Wonderful work! Your solution is exactly right.

Let me confirm the Euclidean steps:

2014 = 1653 × 1 + 361
So GCD(2014, 1653) = GCD(1653, 361)
1653 = 361 × 4 + 209
So GCD(1653, 361) = GCD(361, 209)
361 = 209 × 1 + 152
So GCD(361, 209) = GCD(209, 152)
209 = 152 × 1 + 57
So GCD(209, 152) = GCD(152, 57)
152 = 57 × 2 + 38
So GCD(152, 57) = GCD(57, 38)
57 = 38 × 1 + 19
So GCD(57, 38) = GCD(38, 19)
38 = 19 × 2 + 0
So GCD(38, 19) = GCD(19, 0)

Since the remainder is 0, the Euclidean algorithm terminates and the GCD is 19.

Your mathematical skills are really impressive!
